source_title: Global Planning for Contact-Rich Manipulation via Local Smoothing of Quasi-dynamic Contact Models

ai_summary.one_liner: Global planner for contact-rich manipulation that smooths quasi-dynamic contact models and uses the resulting local models to guide RRT search
ai_summary.problem: Contact-rich manipulation is hard for classical planning because contact modes are combinatorial, non-smooth, and globally non-convex.
ai_summary.solution: Smooth quasi-dynamic contact models locally, derive contact-aware metrics and extension steps, and plug them into an enhanced RRT planner.
ai_summary.key_result: Demonstrates global planning across in-hand manipulation, plate pickup, door opening, pen placement, and bimanual bucket rotation, with lower computation than RL-style approaches.
limitations: Quasi-dynamic first-order model can mismatch second-order real dynamics.
3D systems show larger sim-to-real error than simpler planar setups.
Contact sampling can introduce non-physical teleportation assumptions.

research_gaps.question: 현재는 planner 중심 결과라, closed-loop tactile feedback이나 modern policy/VLA와의 결합은 아직 열려 있다.
